headline: GOLD SPIKES to $4,850, Silver SURGES 7% — US-Iran Ceasefire Triggers Sharpest Dollar Reversal Since Conflict Began
snippet: Gold surged to $4,850/oz on April 8 before settling at $4,743 as a US-Iran two-week ceasefire sent oil below $100/bbl for the first time since late-February, reversing the dollar and lifting precious metals. Silver hit $77/oz — its third consecutive weekly gain.

headline: HISTORIC: NexGen and Denison Both Win CNSC Approval — Canada's First New Uranium Mines Greenlit Since 2004
snippet: NexGen Energy's Rook I project and Denison Mines' Wheeler River both received CNSC construction licences within weeks of each other — the first Canadian uranium mine approvals in 22 years. Combined, 1,600+ construction jobs and $32.5B in economic impact expected for Saskatchewan.

headline: Dryden Gold DRILLS 252 g/t Au Over 0.50m — Three New High-Grade Zones Discovered at Big Master, Gold Rock
snippet: TSX-V junior Dryden Gold hit DGR-048: 4.25m @ 32.87 g/t Au incl. 0.50m @ 252 g/t Au at the new Sparrow zone — a 300m step-out from known mineralization. Two additional holes confirmed the Ruby and BM1 zones at Gold Rock, Ontario, extending the Big Master Gold System further northeast.

headline: Gold Terra (YGT) Drills 22.46 g/t Au Over 5.2m in NWT — Bonanza-Grade 198 g/t Over 0.5m Interval
snippet: Gold Terra Resource Corp (TSX-V: YGT) reported hole GTWL26-024 intersecting 22.46 g/t Au over 5.2 metres at Walsh Lake, NWT, including 198 g/t Au over 0.5 metres. The project sits within the prolific Yellowknife Gold Belt near the former Giant and Con mines.

headline: Americas Gold & Silver UNCOVERS 10 New Veins at Galena — 4,896 g/t Ag, 3.95% Cu in Largest Campaign Ever
snippet: Americas Gold and Silver (TSX: USA) discovered 10 new high-grade silver-copper-antimony and silver-lead veins at its Galena Complex in Idaho, grading up to 4,896 g/t Ag and 3.95% Cu over 1.3m. The company has launched its largest-ever 64,000-metre drill campaign across Idaho and Mexico.

headline: COPPER BREAKS $13,000/Tonne — AI Data Centres to Consume 475,000t in 2026, Escondida Output Drops 7%
snippet: Copper surged above $13,000/t as AI-driven electrification demand accelerates sharply. J.P. Morgan estimates data centres alone will require 475,000 metric tonnes in 2026, while BHP's Escondida — the world's largest mine — saw quarterly output fall 7.4% to 105,000 tonnes, tightening supply.

headline: Lithium Carbonate DOUBLES to $26,278/t in Q1 2026 — Energy Storage Now 31% of Global Demand
snippet: Battery-grade lithium carbonate nearly doubled to US$26,278/t in Q1 2026 on supply delays and speculative buying. Energy storage now accounts for 31% of overall lithium consumption — up from 23% in 2025 — as grid-scale battery deployments accelerate globally.

headline: Coeur Mining CLOSES $7B New Gold Acquisition — 7 North American Mines, $2B Free Cash Flow Guided for 2026
snippet: Coeur Mining completed its acquisition of New Gold Inc., adding the Rainy River and New Afton mines to create a seven-operation all-North-American precious metals producer. The combined entity targets $3B EBITDA and $2B free cash flow in 2026, with a pro forma market cap of ~$20 billion.

headline: China's Zijin Mining BIDS C$5.5B for Allied Gold — All-Cash $44/Share Sweep of Three African Producing Mines
snippet: Zijin Mining announced a C$5.5 billion ($4B USD) all-cash bid for Allied Gold at C$44/share, targeting three producing mines across Mali, Côte d'Ivoire, and Ethiopia. The deal, expected to close Q2 2026, extends China's aggressive accumulation of African gold production capacity.

headline: Mining M&A HIT 13-Year High of $93.7B in 2025 — Mega Deals Up 68%, Momentum Accelerating Into 2026
snippet: Global mining M&A reached $93.7 billion in 2025 — the highest since 2012 — with billion-dollar mega-deals rising 68% from 2024. January 2026 opened strong with 85 transactions worth $11B+, driven by copper scarcity, gold's bull market, and strategic competition for battery metal reserves.

headline: US SECTION 232: Uranium Declared National Security Asset — Price Floors, Import Curbs, Govt Equity All On Table
snippet: Section 232, finalised January 2026, formally designates uranium a national security asset — opening the door to domestic price floors, import restrictions on Russian and Kazakh supplies, and potential government equity stakes in US producers. Spot uranium surpassed $100/lb in January.

headline: Junior Miners OUTRAN Gold in 2026 — GDXJ Up 120%, GDX Up 110% vs. 52% for Bullion; Valuation Gap Closing?
snippet: Junior gold miners (GDXJ) returned 120% and senior miners (GDX) 110% over the past year vs. 52% for spot gold. With AISC around $1,300/oz and gold near $4,750, margins have tripled since 2022 — yet many juniors still trade at deep discounts relative to their US major peers.

headline: JPMorgan Eyes $5,000 Gold, Reuters Poll Median $4,746 — Analysts CONVERGE on Sustained Precious Metals Bull Run
snippet: J.P. Morgan has high conviction gold will reach $5,000/oz in 2026, while a Reuters poll of 30 analysts placed the median 2026 gold forecast at $4,746.50 and silver at $79.50/oz. Central bank accumulation, dollar weakness, and geopolitical hedging demand are cited as durable multi-year tailwinds.
